subject: Re: Warp Threshold_picks Question
body:
Hey!
The cc volumes are normalised so that the background is mean 0, std 1 - thresholds can be interpreted as “number of standard deviations” 
Be careful when choosing —peak_distance - a lot complaints about the performance of template matching are actually great peaks being excluded because of this parameter :-)
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Jun 16, 2025, at 08:36, 'Kyle Messina' via Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:
﻿Hello again,
For the threshold_picks command, what exactly is being used as a threshold to determine what particles are included or excluded?  I assume its using some physical property (like how well it matches a template) but its not specified in the tutorial and I haven't been able to find searching around.  I really just want to know so I have a better idea of why particles are being excluded when I input a number.
Thank you,
Kyle
